content.problemStatement:
This problem has subtasks.

For a string SSS containing only the characters `'a'`, `'b'`, and `'c'`, we define f(S)f(S)f(S) as follows.

Consider the following operation:


Choose any subsequence of SSS that equals abc\texttt{abc}abc, and delete either the `'a'` or the `'b'` from it. Note that this reduces the length of SSS by 111.

Formally, choose indices i,j,ki, j, ki,j,k (1≤i<j<k≤∣S∣1 \leq i \lt j \lt k \leq |S|1≤i<j<k≤∣S∣) such that Si=a,Sj=b,Sk=cS_i = \text{a}, S_j = \text{b}, S_k = \text{c}Si​=a,Sj​=b,Sk​=c, and delete either index iii or index jjj from SSS.
f(S)f(S)f(S) is defined to be the **minimum** number of moves you can make such that it's impossible to perform any further moves on the resulting string.

For example, f(abbc)=1f(\texttt{abbc}) = 1f(abbc)=1 and f(bacacbabbaa)=0f(\texttt{bacacbabbaa}) = 0f(bacacbabbaa)=0.

You are given a string SSS of length NNN. Each character of SSS is either `'a'`, `'b'`, or `'c'`.

Let S[L…R]S[L\ldots R]S[L…R] denote the substring of SSS from index LLL to index RRR, i.e, the string SLSL+1…SRS_LS_{L+1}\ldots S_RSL​SL+1​…SR​.

Find

∑L=1N∑R=LNf(S[L…R])\sum_{L=1}^N \sum_{R=L}^N f(S[L\ldots R])L=1∑N​R=L∑N​f(S[L…R])
Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the length of the string.
The second line of each test case contains a string SSS of length NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the required answer.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.
**Subtask 1 (40 points):** The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 200020002000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Out of all the substrings of abbc\texttt{abbc}abbc, it can be shown that f(abbc)=1f(\texttt{abbc}) = 1f(abbc)=1 and everything else is 000.
So, the sum across all substrings is 111.
Test case 333: Taking a few random substrings as an example, f(bbbcc)=0,f(aabbbc)=2,f(aaabbbcc)=3f(\texttt{bbbcc}) = 0, f(\texttt{aabbbc}) = 2, f(\texttt{aaabbbcc}) = 3f(bbbcc)=0,f(aabbbc)=2,f(aaabbbcc)=3.
There are 454545 substrings in total, it can be shown that the sum of their values is 181818.
